_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Scan, identify and prioritize target graphic (including machine-to-machine communications) and/or voice language material.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of computer vision task taxonomy, including image classification, object detection, segmentation, pose estimation, and generation, and the architectures appropriate for each.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI AI domain. AI-K-013 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AI-S-014 (AI, cosine: 0.414), RM-T-004 (RM, cosine: 0.390).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.5/10 (raw 0.352). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [communications, graphic, identify, language, machine, material] vs WIDAI-only terms [appropriate, architectures, classification, computer, detection, estimation] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.843 (P=0.847, R=0.840). Balanced P/R — the 13-word DCWF element and 22-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (97.8%). "Scan, identify and prioritize target graphic (including machine-to-machine commu" and "Knowledge of computer vision task taxonomy, including image classification, obje" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.415 > STS 0.352 (gap +0.063). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 6.1%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [communications, graphic, identify, language, machine, material]. WIDAI terms: [appropriate, architectures, classification, computer, detection, estimation].
